title: cassia stenocarpoides
intro: Cassia stenocarpoides, also known as the shrubby senna, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family that is native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its laxative and anti-inflammatory effects, due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of Cassia stenocarpoides include treating constipation, diarrhea, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as reducing fever and inflammation. Cassia stenocarpoides is often prepared as a herbal tea or dried root powder, which can be used to make capsules or added to other herbal remedies for its medicinal properties.
